title: Web color-system fallback for older browsers (oklch/@supports) + admin browser gate

tasks.title: Write failing regression guards (RED — guard before fix)
tasks.description:
Per CLAUDE.md bug-fix rule: regression guards land FIRST.

Create TWO guards:

**Guard 1 — Vitest unit test (RED):**
Create `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-coverage.test.ts`.
The test imports `buildCSS()` from `generate-css.ts` and `VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP` from `variant-tokens.ts` (these files do not yet contain variant logic — the test will FAIL because VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP will be empty and no variant tokens will be in CSS output).

Three `it` blocks:
1. 'every variant token has a non-oklch sRGB :root declaration' — for each entry in VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP, regex-assert `--{name}: rgb(` appears in generated CSS outside `@supports`.
2. 'every variant token has an oklch declaration inside @supports' — extract the `@supports (color: oklch(from white l c h))` block from the CSS; assert each `--{name}:` with `oklch(from var(` appears inside it.
3. 'no variant token name collides with existing palette or theme tokens' — assert the generated names are unique against all non-variant token names.

Start with VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP as an empty array — all three tests PASS trivially. Mark TODO comment: 'SPEC-176: populate VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP in T-002 to make guard meaningful'.

**Guard 2 — CI lint script (scaffold):**
Create `apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs`.
The script walks `apps/web/src/` for `.astro`, `.css`, `.tsx`, `.ts` files, strips block comments, scans for `oklch(from`, and exits 1 if ANY match is found (zero allowlist for now — the web source CURRENTLY has 679 violations, so this script will fail hard until T-006 completes. That is intentional: it runs in CI AFTER the codemod lands, not before).

Add the script invocation to `apps/web/package.json` under a `'check:relative-colors'` script (but do NOT wire to CI in turbo.json yet — that happens in T-013).

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm test` in packages/design-tokens: the coverage test file exists and all 3 `it` blocks run (trivially pass with empty map)
- `node apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs` exits 1 (correctly detects the 679 violations in current source)
- Script prints a summary: 'Found N oklch(from occurrences in M files. Fix: run codemod-relative-colors.mjs'
- No typecheck errors introduced

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-coverage.test.ts` (new)
- `apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs` (new)
- `apps/web/package.json` (add script entry)

tasks.title: Fix alien token-count baseline in design-tokens (restore green)
tasks.description:
Restore design-tokens test suite to GREEN by correcting 6 hardcoded token-count assertions that drifted due to ALIEN commits (sponsors/amenities/auth/post-categories icon+color SSOT work) that were merged before SPEC-176 and grew the theme records without updating the counts.

The 6 failing tests and their corrections:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/themes/web.test.ts` — webLight count: 155 → 208
- `packages/design-tokens/src/index.test.ts` — webLight count: 155 → 208
- `packages/design-tokens/src/themes/admin.test.ts` — adminLight count: 39 → 92
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/generate-css.test.ts` — 3 failures:
  - :root total count: 305 → 358
  - admin count: 39 → 92
  - full-output snapshot regression in `__snapshots__/generate-css.test.ts.snap`

What to do:
1. Update the 4 hardcoded numeric count asserts in the 3 test files to their real current values (208, 208, 92, 358, 92).
2. Regenerate the generate-css snapshot by running `pnpm --filter @repo/design-tokens test -- --update-snapshots` (or the project-equivalent snapshot-update command). Commit the updated `.snap` file.
3. Do NOT change any source files or theme definitions — only the test asserts and the snapshot file change.

Note explicitly in the commit message that the count drift is ALIEN debt from prior sponsor/amenity/auth SSOT commits, so the SPEC-176 PR description can call it out. T-004 (emitVariantTokens, adds 42 variant tokens) will shift these counts again and re-regenerate the snapshot later — that is expected.

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/design-tokens test` exits 0 (0 failures, all 6 previously-red tests now GREEN)
- `pnpm typecheck` clean in packages/design-tokens
- No source or theme files changed — only test assert values and the `.snap` file
- Commit message references 'alien baseline drift' and SPEC-176

tasks.title: Add culori devDependency and implement formatSRGB() in design-tokens
tasks.description:
Install `culori` as a devDependency on `packages/design-tokens` and implement the `formatSRGB()` converter function.

**Step 1 — Install:**
Add `culori` to `packages/design-tokens/package.json` under `devDependencies`. This is a build-time-only dependency. It is NOT added to any consumer package.

**Step 2 — Implement formatSRGB() in `packages/design-tokens/src/tokens/colors.ts`:**
Add immediately after the existing `formatOKLCH()` function:

```ts
/**
 * Convert an OKLCH value to a CSS `rgb(R G B)` string via sRGB gamut-mapped
 * conversion using culori's CSS Color Level 4 clampChroma algorithm.
 * Used at build time by the design-tokens generator (SPEC-176).
 * Components are rounded to integers after gamut mapping.
 *
 * @param value - OKLCH triple {l, c, h} to convert.
 * @returns CSS `rgb(R G B)` string with space-separated integer components (no alpha — caller appends / ALPHA).
 */
export function formatSRGB(value: OKLCH): string {
    // culori import is dynamic to avoid bundling at consumer sites
    const { oklch, formatRgb, clampChroma } = require('culori');
    const mapped = clampChroma({ mode: 'oklch', l: value.l, c: value.c, h: value.h }, 'oklch', 'p3');
    const rgb = formatRgb(mapped);
    // formatRgb returns 'rgb(R, G, B)' — normalize to space-syntax CSS
    return rgb.replace(/,/g, '').replace('rgb(', 'rgb(');
}
```

Note: use `require('culori')` inside the function body (CJS interop) or an ESM dynamic import if the generator runs under `tsx`. Match whichever module resolution pattern `generate-css.ts` already uses.

**Step 3 — Unit test for formatSRGB:**
Add a describe block to the coverage test file from T-001:
- Test `formatSRGB({ l: 0.63, c: 0.19, h: 259 })` returns a string matching `/^rgb\(\d+ \d+ \d+\)$/`
- Test a known in-gamut value (e.g. `{l: 0.5, c: 0.0, h: 0}` → mid-gray) returns approximately `rgb(128 128 128)`
- Test a high-chroma out-of-gamut value does NOT return negative component values (gamut mapping correctly clamps)

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm build:css` in packages/design-tokens succeeds (no errors importing culori)
- New formatSRGB unit tests pass
- formatSRGB output is a valid CSS rgb() string with space-separated integers
- culori is NOT in the production dependency list of any other package

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/package.json` (devDependencies)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/tokens/colors.ts` (add formatSRGB)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-coverage.test.ts` (add formatSRGB unit tests)

AC mapping: PDR Rule 5 (deterministic derivation from canonical token values).

Implement formatSRGB() in colors.ts with JSDoc

Verify pnpm build:css succeeds

tasks.title: Define VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP with all 138 entries and Zod schema
tasks.description:
Derive and codify the complete VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP constant and its Zod schema.

**Step 1 — Derive the 42 entries:**
Run the census script at `/tmp/beta44-oklch-census2.mjs` (or re-derive from the audit data in spec.md §2) to extract the exact 42 `(base, transform, replaces)` triplets. The census confirms:
- ~27 alpha-only entries: base tokens × distinct alpha values
- ~15 lightness-math entries (both `calc(l * N)` multiply and `calc(l - N)` subtract families)

Naming convention (D6 — locked):
- Alpha: `--{base}-a{NN}` where NN is the alpha percentage (0.15 → `a15`, 0.05 → `a05`)
- Lightness multiply: `--{base}-l{NN}` where NN = factor × 100 (0.85 → `l85`)
- Lightness subtract: `--{base}-lm{NN}` where NN = offset × 100 (0.20 → `lm20`)
- Combined: `--{base}-l85-a15`

**Step 2 — Create `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-tokens.ts`:**
```ts
import type { VariantTokenEntry } from './variant-token-schema.ts';

export const VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP: ReadonlyArray<VariantTokenEntry> = [
    // Alpha family — 625 usages normalized to ~27 (base x alpha) pairs
    { name: 'brand-primary-a15', base: 'brand-primary', family: 'alpha', param: 0.15, replaces: 'oklch(from var(--brand-primary) l c h / 0.15)' },
    { name: 'brand-primary-a30', base: 'brand-primary', family: 'alpha', param: 0.30, replaces: 'oklch(from var(--brand-primary) l c h / 0.3)' },
    // ... all 42 entries derived from census
    // Lightness-multiply family
    { name: 'brand-primary-l85', base: 'brand-primary', family: 'lightness-multiply', param: 0.85, replaces: 'oklch(from var(--brand-primary) calc(l * 0.85) c h)' },
    // Lightness-subtract family
    { name: 'core-background-lm20', base: 'core-background', family: 'lightness-subtract', param: 0.20, replaces: 'oklch(from var(--core-background) calc(l - 0.2) c h)' },
    // ...
] as const;
```

**Step 3 — Create `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-schema.ts`:**
The Zod schema from tech-analysis §4 exactly. The schema validates:
- name: matches `/^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$/`
- base: same regex
- family: `z.enum(['alpha', 'lightness-multiply', 'lightness-subtract'])`
- param: `z.number().finite()`
- replaces: non-empty string
- Two refinements: no duplicate names, no duplicate replaces strings

**Step 4 — Wire schema validation into coverage test:**
Add an `it` block: 'VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP passes Zod schema validation' — parse the full map through `VariantTokenMapSchema` and assert no errors.

Acceptance criteria:
- 42 entries in VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP (exact count verified by a test assertion)
- All names follow D6 convention (regex check in tests)
- VariantTokenMapSchema.parse(VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP) throws for any duplicate name or replaces string
- No TypeScript errors
- Coverage test: Zod validation `it` block passes GREEN

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-tokens.ts` (new)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-schema.ts` (new)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-coverage.test.ts` (add Zod validation test)

AC mapping: PDR Rule 5 (deterministic derivation), PDR Story 1 AC1 (all elements render with brand colors).

Create variant-token-schema.ts with Zod schema + two refinements + lightness-add family
Create variant-tokens.ts + variant-tokens-alpha.ts with all 138 VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP entries
Add Zod validation test + 138-entry count assertion + D6 naming + family breakdown to coverage test

tasks.title: Extend generate-css.ts with emitVariantTokens() and regenerate dist/tokens.css
tasks.description:
Add the CSS emission functions to the design-tokens generator and produce the updated `dist/tokens.css`.

**Step 1 — Add `emitVariantTokens()` to `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/generate-css.ts`:**

The function emits a SINGLE :root block (sRGB fallbacks for ALL 42 tokens), then a SINGLE @supports block with the oklch overrides for all 42 tokens:

```ts
/* SPEC-176: 42 variant tokens — sRGB fallback + @supports oklch override. */
:root {
    --brand-primary-a15: rgb(78 100 210 / 0.15);
    /* ... 41 more ... */
}
@supports (color: oklch(from white l c h)) {
    :root {
        --brand-primary-a15: oklch(from var(--brand-primary) l c h / 0.15);
        /* ... 41 more ... */
    }
}
```

For each entry:
- Alpha family: sRGB = `rgb({formatSRGB(baseOklch)} / {param})`
- Lightness-multiply: compute `{l: base.l * param, c: base.c, h: base.h}`, then `formatSRGB()`, no alpha suffix
- Lightness-subtract: compute `{l: Math.max(0, base.l - param), c: base.c, h: base.h}`, then `formatSRGB()`

The `baseOklchValues` record is derived from the existing `webLight` theme map filtered to OKLCH-typed tokens.

**Step 2 — Integrate into `buildCSS()`:**
Append the variant tokens section at the end of the :root section (after palette, before theme overrides).

**Step 3 — Run the generator:**
```bash
cd packages/design-tokens && pnpm build:css
```
Inspect `dist/tokens.css` to verify:
- The 42 `--{name}: rgb(...)` declarations appear in `:root` BEFORE the @supports block
- The @supports block exists with all 42 oklch redefinitions
- No undefined token references (all bases are present in webLight)

**Step 4 — Make coverage test GREEN:**
With the 42 entries now in VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP and the generator emitting them, the three coverage `it` blocks from T-001 will now be meaningful assertions. Run `pnpm test` in packages/design-tokens — all tests must pass.

Acceptance criteria:
- `dist/tokens.css` contains exactly 42 sRGB fallback declarations + 42 oklch @supports declarations
- All three coverage test `it` blocks pass GREEN (T-001 tests now meaningful)
- No existing tests broken (no token name collisions with existing palette/theme tokens)
- `generate-css.ts` stays under 500 lines; VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP lives in `variant-tokens.ts` (imported)

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/generate-css.ts` (add emitVariantTokens, integrate into buildCSS)
- `packages/design-tokens/dist/tokens.css` (regenerated artifact)

AC mapping: PDR Rule 5, Story 2 AC2 (modern browser resolves via @supports).

tasks.title: Write and run codemod to swap 679 call-sites from inline oklch to var(--token)
tasks.description:
Create and execute the codemod script that replaces all 679 inline `oklch(from var(--...))` expressions in web CSS with `var(--token)` references.

**Step 1 — Create `packages/design-tokens/scripts/codemod-relative-colors.mjs`:**
(Kept in design-tokens since it references VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP)

Logic:
1. Dynamically import VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP from `../src/generators/variant-tokens.ts` (use tsx/esm interop)
2. Collect all target CSS/Astro/TSX files under `apps/web/src/` matching `.css`, `.astro`, `.tsx`
3. For each file, build the replacement table from VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP `replaces` → `var(--{name})`
4. Apply ALL replacements (loop through all 42 entries with `String.replaceAll`)
5. Write file back only if changed (avoid unnecessary dirty state)
6. After substitution, scan each file for any remaining `oklch(from var(` patterns
7. Write `packages/design-tokens/scripts/codemod-report.md` listing unmatched occurrences with file:line references

**Step 2 — Run the codemod:**
```bash
node --loader tsx packages/design-tokens/scripts/codemod-relative-colors.mjs
```

**Step 3 — Analyze codemod-report.md:**
The ~49 lightness-math cases (and any edge cases) will appear in the report. For each:
- If a clean token exists in VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP: verify the substitution happened (likely the replaces string was slightly different — fix the VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP entry in T-003 and re-run)
- If no token covers it: add a new entry to VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP, regenerate CSS (T-004), re-run codemod
- If it's a genuinely non-tokenizable expression: inline a static sRGB hex approximation with a TODO comment (per tech-analysis §14 — acceptable transition approach)

**Step 4 — Generalize components.css:539 existing @supports block:**
Delete the hand-crafted `@supports` block at `apps/web/src/styles/components.css:539`. Verify that `--core-card-a95` token from VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP covers the `.navbar--scrolled` background-color usage.

Acceptance criteria:
- `codemod-report.md` exists and lists all unresolved usages
- Zero `oklch(from var(` patterns remain in ANY web CSS/Astro/TSX file EXCEPT inside the generated `dist/tokens.css`
- The existing @supports block at components.css:539 is deleted and replaced by the token reference
- `pnpm check` (Biome lint) passes on all modified files
- Files in report are manually resolved (added to map or hex-inlined with TODO)

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/scripts/codemod-relative-colors.mjs` (new)
- `apps/web/src/styles/components.css` (679 replacements + @supports block deletion)
- `apps/web/src/styles/global.css` (1 replacement: outline-color at line 29)
- Potentially other `.astro`/`.tsx` files in apps/web/src/ if any contain inline usages

AC mapping: PDR Story 1 AC1 AC3 AC4 (all pages render with brand colors on Chrome 109), Rule 1 (sRGB fallback for old browsers).

tasks.title: Fix @repo/icons subtle variant to emit var(--token) instead of raw oklch string
tasks.description:
Update the 6 domain files in `packages/icons/src/domain/` so the `subtle` variant emits a CSS custom property reference (`var(--base-aNN)`) rather than an inline `oklch(from var(--token) l c h / 0.15)` string.

**Step 1 — Create `alphaTokenName()` helper in `packages/icons/src/`:**
Create `packages/icons/src/oklch-color-helper.ts`:

```ts
/**
 * Constructs the variant token CSS custom property name for a base token + alpha value.
 * Follows the SPEC-176 naming convention: --{base}-a{NN}.
 * Example: alphaTokenName('brand-primary', 0.15) -> 'var(--brand-primary-a15)'
 *
 * @param base - Base token name without dashes (e.g. 'brand-primary')
 * @param alpha - Alpha value as decimal (0.15, 0.30)
 * @returns CSS var() reference string for use as inline style value
 */
export function alphaTokenName(base: string, alpha: number): string {
    const nn = String(Math.round(alpha * 100)).padStart(2, '0');
    return `var(--${base}-a${nn})`;
}
```

**Step 2 — Update all 6 affected domain files:**
For each of the 6 domain files (accommodation, destination, event, post, user, service — verify exact filenames from `packages/icons/src/domain/`):

In the `getXxxColorScheme()` function, the `subtle` variant currently emits:
```ts
bg: `oklch(from var(--${token}) l c h / 0.15)`,
border: `oklch(from var(--${token}) l c h / 0.3)`,
```

Change to:
```ts
bg: alphaTokenName(token, 0.15),
border: alphaTokenName(token, 0.30),
```

The `contrast` variant (admin-only, Chrome 111+ floor) is intentionally left unchanged. Add a code comment:
```ts
// contrast variant uses raw oklch relative-color — admin-only, Chrome 111+ floor (SPEC-176 intentional).
```

**Step 3 — Update icon tests:**
If the domain files have tests asserting the old oklch string output, update them to assert the new `var(--token-aNN)` output.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 6 domain files emit `var(--{base}-aNN)` for the subtle bg and border properties
- `pnpm typecheck` passes in packages/icons
- All existing icon tests pass (updated expected values)
- The contrast variant is unchanged and documented
- On a modern browser (CSS var resolves via @supports oklch); on Chrome 109 (CSS var resolves to sRGB fallback at :root)

Files affected:
- `packages/icons/src/oklch-color-helper.ts` (new)
- `packages/icons/src/domain/*.ts` (6 files, subtle variant change)
- Icon test files if they assert color values

AC mapping: PDR Story 4 AC1 AC2 AC3 (badge has visible background on Chrome 109; modern browser parity).

tasks.title: Create BrowserGateBanner component and mount in admin root
tasks.description:
Implement the admin browser-gate banner React component and wire it into the root layout.

**Step 1 — Create `apps/admin/src/components/BrowserGateBanner.tsx`:**

Key implementation details (D4 + D8 locked decisions):
- Detection uses CSS.supports() for TWO probes (NOT UA sniffing per PDR Rule 3):
  ```ts
  const supported =
      typeof CSS !== 'undefined' &&
      CSS.supports('color', 'oklch(from white l c h)') &&
      CSS.supports('color', 'color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue)');
  ```
- Dismissibility: SESSION-dismissible via sessionStorage (not localStorage per D8)
  - On mount: check `sessionStorage.getItem('browserGateDismissed')` — if truthy, skip render
  - Dismiss button stores `sessionStorage.setItem('browserGateDismissed', '1')`
- Detection runs in `useEffect` (client-side only; SSR safe)
- Banner is `null` if browser is supported or session-dismissed
- Uses `role="alert"` and `aria-live="polite"` per PDR §5 accessibility requirements
- The dismiss button has `aria-label` from `t('admin.browserGate.dismiss')`
- Uses INLINE STYLES for critical colors (not Tailwind) so the banner is legible on Chrome 109 where Tailwind oklch may break:
  ```tsx
  style={{ backgroundColor: '#fef3c7', borderColor: '#d97706', color: '#78350f' }}
  ```
  Tailwind classes serve as progressive enhancement for modern browsers
- Two CTAs: 'Descargar Chrome' → `https://www.google.com/chrome/` (new tab), 'Contactar soporte' → `mailto:soporte@hospeda.com.ar` (or configured URL)
- Uses `useTranslations()` hook from `@repo/i18n` for all copy

**Step 2 — Mount in `apps/admin/src/routes/__root.tsx`:**
Add `<BrowserGateBanner />` as the FIRST child inside the root component body, before the main layout structure. Named export only (no default export).

**Step 3 — Write component unit test:**
Create `apps/admin/src/components/__tests__/BrowserGateBanner.test.tsx`:
- Test: when CSS.supports returns false for both probes, banner renders with role='alert'
- Test: when CSS.supports returns true, banner renders null
- Test: when sessionStorage has 'browserGateDismissed'='1', banner renders null even with unsupported browser
- Test: clicking dismiss button sets sessionStorage and removes banner
- Mock CSS.supports via vitest `vi.stubGlobal`

Acceptance criteria:
- Banner appears on unsupported browser (CSS.supports=false), disappears on supported browser
- Session-dismissible: dismiss click sets sessionStorage flag, banner gone; page reload restores it
- All 4 unit tests pass
- `pnpm typecheck` clean in apps/admin
- No UA string parsing anywhere in the component
- CTA links have correct href + target='_blank' + rel='noopener noreferrer'

Files affected:
- `apps/admin/src/components/BrowserGateBanner.tsx` (new)
- `apps/admin/src/components/__tests__/BrowserGateBanner.test.tsx` (new)
- `apps/admin/src/routes/__root.tsx` (mount banner)

AC mapping: PDR Story 3 all ACs, Rule 2, Rule 3, Rule 4, §5 (dismissibility, accessibility, copy).

tasks.title: Verify dark-mode token fallback correctness
tasks.description:
Validate that the dark-mode base token overrides (`[data-theme='dark']`) produce correct sRGB fallbacks on old browsers — that variant tokens resolve to dark-mode values in both the fallback and modern paths.

**Context:**
The design-token generator emits variant tokens relative to the LIGHT theme base token values (what `webLight` resolves). In dark mode the base tokens are overridden via `[data-theme='dark'] { --brand-primary: oklch(0.7 0.2 260); }`. Since the VARIANT tokens reference `oklch(from var(--brand-primary) ...)` inside @supports, they automatically inherit the dark-mode override on modern browsers.

On Chrome 109 (sRGB fallback path):
- The sRGB fallback `:root { --brand-primary-a15: rgb(78 100 210 / 0.15); }` uses the LIGHT-mode sRGB value
- BUT the dark-mode override of the BASE token does NOT cascade to the VARIANT token's :root declaration
- This means dark-mode users on Chrome 109 get LIGHT-mode variant colors — a known limitation

**What this task does:**
1. Read `apps/web/src/styles/global.css` and `css-var-themes.css` to confirm which base tokens have dark-mode overrides and whether those overrides affect variant token resolution
2. Enumerate how many variant tokens have base tokens with dark-mode overrides (likely: --brand-primary, --brand-accent, --core-background, --core-card, etc.)
3. Decide: is the light-mode fallback for dark-mode users on Chrome 109 acceptable, or must dark-mode sRGB fallbacks also be generated?

**If dark-mode fallbacks are needed (adds ~15 extra entries):**
Extend VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP to include a `darkModeOklch` optional field and extend `emitVariantTokens()` to also emit a `[data-theme='dark'] { :root { } }` block with the dark-mode sRGB values.

**If acceptable degradation:**
Document explicitly in code comments that dark-mode on Chrome 109 uses light-mode variant colors (not a security or functionality issue; purely cosmetic).

Write a test that asserts the dark-mode degradation behavior is documented:
```ts
it('dark-mode on Chrome 109 uses light-mode sRGB fallback — documented intentional degradation', () => {
    // This test exists to document the behavior, not to fix it.
    // On Chrome 109 [data-theme='dark'] overrides the BASE token but not the :root VARIANT token.
    // See SPEC-176 tech-analysis §14 (future improvement).
    expect(true).toBe(true); // Intentional no-op — documentation anchor
});
```

Acceptance criteria:
- PDR Edge Case 3 (dark mode on old browser) behavior is explicitly understood and documented
- If dark-mode fallbacks are generated: emitVariantTokens() emits additional [data-theme='dark'] block; coverage test asserts its presence
- If documented degradation: code comment + test documentation anchor exists
- Decision recorded in code comment in generate-css.ts

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/generate-css.ts` (possibly extend emitVariantTokens)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-token-coverage.test.ts` (add dark-mode test)

AC mapping: PDR Story 1 AC5 (dark mode fallback respect), Edge Case 3.

subtasks.title: Audit dark-mode base token overrides in global.css + css-var-themes.css
Enumerate variant tokens whose base has dark-mode overrides
Decide: generate dark-mode sRGB fallbacks OR document acceptable degradation
Implement decision (emit dark-mode block or add doc comment + test anchor)

tasks.title: Run Playwright visual parity check post-codemod (verify zero regression on modern browser)
tasks.description:
Run the existing Playwright visual snapshot suite to confirm the codemod introduced zero visual regression on modern browsers.

**Context:**
The Playwright visual baseline suite is at `apps/web/tests/visual-snapshots/capture-baseline.visual.ts` (from SPEC-153 Phase 0). It covers 8 pages × 4 viewports × 2 themes = 64 baseline PNGs.

**Step 1 — Run WITHOUT --update-snapshots:**
```bash
cd apps/web && npx playwright test --config=playwright-visual.config.ts
```
Expected: all 64 snapshots pass with zero pixel diff (the modern browser uses @supports path = identical oklch values).

**Step 2 — Investigate any diffs:**
If diffs appear:
- Check which token changed
- Compare the sRGB fallback in dist/tokens.css vs the original oklch value
- Determine if it's a gamut-boundary issue (high-chroma token: --destructive, --brand-accent)
- Adjust gamut mapping strategy if needed (culori clampChroma should handle this correctly)

**Step 3 — Update baselines ONLY if diffs are intentional:**
If a color difference is a verified gamut-mapping result (i.e. the sRGB approximation is visually close but not pixel-identical due to P3 gamut), and the design team approves it, run with `--update-snapshots` and commit the new baselines with an explicit comment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Playwright test run completes against a local dev server serving the post-codemod web build
- Zero unexpected pixel diffs (either 0 diff or documented intentional baseline updates)
- PDR Story 2 verified: modern browser sees zero visual difference
- If any token produces a diff > 0: root cause documented and either fixed or baseline updated with approval

Files affected:
- `apps/web/tests/visual-snapshots/*.png` (potentially updated baselines)
- `apps/web/tests/visual-snapshots/capture-baseline.visual.ts` (no code change — run only)

AC mapping: PDR Story 2 all ACs (zero visual regression), Rule 1 (fallback colors are visually equivalent), §9 success metrics (regression rate = 0).

subtasks.title: Start local apps/web dev server with post-codemod build
Run Playwright visual suite without --update-snapshots
Investigate any diffs — identify gamut-boundary tokens
Either fix gamut mapping or update baselines with documented approval

tasks.title: Add check-css-relative-colors.cjs to turbo.json CI pipeline
tasks.description:
Wire the CI guard script from T-001 into the TurboRepo CI pipeline so it runs on every PR after the codemod is complete (T-005).

**Step 1 — Update `apps/web/package.json`:**
Ensure the `'check:relative-colors'` script added in T-001 is correct and covers all relevant extensions.

**Step 2 — Update `turbo.json` (root):**
Add the guard script as a task in the `apps/web` pipeline under the lint/check phase:
```json
"check:relative-colors": {
    "cache": false,
    "inputs": ["src/**/*.css", "src/**/*.astro", "src/**/*.tsx", "src/**/*.ts"],
    "outputs": []
}
```
Alternatively, add it as a step in the existing lint task dependency chain so it runs when `pnpm lint` runs for apps/web.

**Step 3 — Verify the script passes on post-codemod source:**
After T-005 is complete, run the script:
```bash
node apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs
```
Expected: exits 0 (zero `oklch(from` occurrences in web source files).

**Step 4 — Verify the script correctly fails on a regression:**
Temporarily add `oklch(from var(--brand-primary) l c h / 0.1)` to a CSS file, run the script, verify exit 1, then revert.

Acceptance criteria:
- `node apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs` exits 0 on post-codemod source
- The script is wired to run in CI (turbo.json or npm script chain)
- The script exits 1 and reports the file + line if any `oklch(from` appears in web source
- PDR Edge Case 6 (guard false-positive prevention) verified: the allowlist correctly exempts `dist/tokens.css` (a generated artifact, not in src/)

Files affected:
- `turbo.json` (add pipeline entry for check:relative-colors)
- `apps/web/package.json` (verify/update script)

AC mapping: PDR §9 success metrics (CI guard catches new violations), Edge Case 6 (no false positives on token definition file).

subtasks.title: Add check:relative-colors task to turbo.json pipeline
Verify script exits 0 on post-codemod web source
Verify script exits 1 on a temporarily-introduced oklch(from regression
Confirm dist/tokens.css (generated) is not in scan scope (no false positive)

tasks.title: Run full test suite and integration smoke — all GREEN
tasks.description:
Final integration check: all unit tests, the coverage guard, and a local smoke of the critical paths pass before opening a PR.

**Step 1 — Run full test suites:**
```bash
pnpm test  # root — all packages + apps
```
Expected passing:
- `packages/design-tokens`: variant-token-coverage.test.ts (all 3+ it blocks GREEN)
- `packages/icons`: domain file tests (var(--token) assertions)
- `apps/admin`: BrowserGateBanner.test.tsx (4 tests)
- All pre-existing tests (no regressions introduced)

**Step 2 — Typecheck all affected packages:**
```bash
pnpm typecheck
```
Affected packages: design-tokens, icons, i18n, apps/admin, apps/web.

**Step 3 — Run CI guard locally:**
```bash
node apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs
```
Expected: exits 0.

**Step 4 — Local smoke of critical paths:**
```bash
pnpm dev:web  # or equivalent
```
- Navigate to `/es/auth/signin/` — verify GradientButton has a visible background
- Navigate to a page with entity badges — verify badges are visible
- Toggle dark mode — verify colors are reasonable
- Open admin — verify BrowserGateBanner does NOT appear on modern browser (Chrome 119+)

**Step 5 — Admin banner smoke (requires DevTools override):**
In Chrome DevTools, override `CSS.supports` to return false for the oklch probe:
```js
Object.defineProperty(CSS, 'supports', { value: () => false });
```
Reload admin — banner should appear. Dismiss it — banner hides. Reload page — banner reappears (sessionStorage cleared on DevTools navigation to different origin... confirm behavior).

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm test` passes with 90%+ coverage on new code
- `pnpm typecheck` exits 0
- CI guard exits 0
- Manual smoke: login button visible, badges visible, admin banner behavior correct

Files affected: none (test-run only)

AC mapping: PDR Story 1 (web renders with brand colors), Story 2 (no regression), Story 3 (admin banner fires correctly), §9 success metrics.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm test — all suites GREEN
Run pnpm typecheck — 0 errors
Run CI guard — exits 0
Local smoke: /es/auth/signin/ GradientButton visible
Local smoke: admin banner via CSS.supports override in DevTools

tasks.title: Wire CI gate, add JSDoc, and update package documentation
tasks.description:
Final integration: confirm the turbo.json CI wiring runs correctly end-to-end, and add all required documentation to new/modified code.

**Step 1 — Verify CI pipeline integration (from T-011):**
Confirm turbo.json correctly sequences `check:relative-colors` after `build` (since dist/tokens.css must exist before the guard runs its allowlist logic). If the guard scans only src/ (not dist/), ordering is less critical — verify and document.

**Step 2 — Add JSDoc to all new exported functions and constants (per CLAUDE.md):**

Functions requiring JSDoc:
- `formatSRGB()` in `packages/design-tokens/src/tokens/colors.ts` — include gamut-mapping note, link to CSS Color 4 spec, SPEC-176 reference
- `emitVariantTokens()` in `generate-css.ts` — describe dual-declaration pattern, SPEC-176 reference
- `VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP` constant — explain `replaces` field and codemod relationship
- `alphaTokenName()` in `packages/icons/src/oklch-color-helper.ts` — describe naming convention
- `check-css-relative-colors.cjs` header — explain allowlist, allowed location for `oklch(from`

**Step 3 — Add architecture comments in web CSS:**
At the top of `apps/web/src/styles/global.css`:
```css
/*
 * SPEC-176: Variant tokens (sRGB fallback + @supports oklch override) are generated
 * by @repo/design-tokens and live in dist/tokens.css (imported via @repo/design-tokens).
 * Do NOT add oklch(from var(...)) inline in this file — use the precomputed var(--token) references.
 */
```

**Step 4 — Update generate-css.ts file header:**
Add the variant token block to the documented output layout description in the file's header comment.

Acceptance criteria:
- All new exported functions have comprehensive JSDoc
- VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP has JSDoc explaining `replaces` field and codemod relationship
- Architecture comment added to global.css header
- generate-css.ts file header updated with variant token block description
- `pnpm lint` clean on all modified files

Files affected:
- `packages/design-tokens/src/tokens/colors.ts` (JSDoc formatSRGB)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/generate-css.ts` (JSDoc emitVariantTokens + header update)
- `packages/design-tokens/src/generators/variant-tokens.ts` (JSDoc VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP)
- `packages/icons/src/oklch-color-helper.ts` (JSDoc alphaTokenName)
- `apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs` (file header comment)
- `apps/web/src/styles/global.css` (architecture comment at top)

AC mapping: PDR §15 documentation requirements.

tasks.title: Commit and open PR to staging — SPEC-176 implementation
tasks.description:
Stage all SPEC-176 changes, commit following conventional commits, and open a PR targeting `staging`.

**Commit strategy (per CLAUDE.md — atomic, files staged individually):**

Suggest 4 atomic commits in this order:
1. `fix(design-tokens): add culori devDep + formatSRGB() + VARIANT_TOKEN_MAP + emitVariantTokens (SPEC-176)`
   Files: packages/design-tokens/package.json, colors.ts, variant-tokens.ts, variant-token-schema.ts, generate-css.ts, dist/tokens.css

2. `fix(web): codemod 679 oklch(from) call-sites to var(--token) (SPEC-176)`
   Files: apps/web/src/styles/components.css, global.css, any other changed web src files

3. `fix(icons): subtle variant emits var(--token) instead of raw oklch string (SPEC-176)`
   Files: packages/icons/src/oklch-color-helper.ts, packages/icons/src/domain/*.ts

4. `fix(admin): browser gate banner for pre-Chrome-111 browsers + i18n + CI guard (SPEC-176)`
   Files: packages/i18n locales, apps/admin/src/components/BrowserGateBanner.tsx, __root.tsx, apps/web/scripts/check-css-relative-colors.cjs, turbo.json

**Before committing:**
- Verify `git status` shows ONLY SPEC-176 related files
- Run `pnpm check` (Biome lint+format) on all changed files
- Run `pnpm typecheck` — 0 errors
- Run `pnpm test` — all suites GREEN

**Open PR:**
```bash
gh pr create --base staging --title 'fix(web): CSS color-system fallback for Chrome 109-118 + admin browser gate (SPEC-176)' --body "..."
```

PR body must reference:
- Linear BETA-44 (root cause, Marta's login)
- 679 call-site swap count
- 42 variant tokens generated
- Browser support: Chrome 109+ (web), Chrome 111+ (admin with gate)
- Testing: variant-token-coverage tests, BrowserGateBanner tests, Playwright parity
- Manual smoke: login button visible, badges visible, admin banner behavior

Acceptance criteria:
- All 4 commits are atomic and follow conventional commits format
- No `git add .` or `git add -A` used (files staged individually per CLAUDE.md)
- PR opens successfully targeting staging
- No AI attribution or co-authorship lines in commits
- CI runs on the PR (even if some pre-existing CI is red from unrelated issues, SPEC-176 changes must not introduce NEW failures)

Files affected: all SPEC-176 modified files (staged individually)

AC mapping: PDR §12 Approval (ready for review), §9 success metrics.

subtasks.title: Run final pnpm check + typecheck + test
Stage and commit 4 atomic commits (files staged individually)
Open PR to staging with SPEC-176 description + BETA-44 reference
Verify CI kicks off on PR

tasks.title: Post-merge: update index files and close BETA-44
tasks.description:
After the PR is merged to staging: update the project registry files and close the Linear issue.

**Step 1 — Update `.qtm/specs/index.json`:**
Flip SPEC-176 `status` → `completed`, add `completedAt` ISO timestamp, add `completionRef: 'PR #<N> (commit <sha>)'`.

**Step 2 — Update `.qtm/tasks/index.json`:**
Flip SPEC-176 entry `status` → `completed`, update `progress` to `15/15`, add `completedAt` and `completionRef`. (Per CLAUDE.md Index Sync Rules — both indexes must be flipped in the same change.)

**Step 3 — Update Engram:**
- `mem_save` with `topic_key: spec/SPEC-176/completed`: PR ref, merge commit, completion date, files affected, BETA-44 closure.

**Step 4 — Close Linear BETA-44:**
Mark Linear BETA-44 as done with a comment linking to the PR and noting the root cause (679 inline oklch(from) usages in Chrome 119+ CSS on a Chrome 109 browser) and the fix (42 variant tokens with sRGB fallbacks + @supports override + 679-site codemod + admin gate).

**Step 5 — Notify BETA-44 reporter:**
Post a comment on BETA-44 asking 'Marta' to retest login on Chrome 109, linking to the staging deployment.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both index files updated and committed
- Engram has spec/SPEC-176/completed observation
- Linear BETA-44 is closed
- Reporter notified

Files affected:
- `.qtm/specs/index.json`
- `.qtm/tasks/index.json`

AC mapping: PDR §9 (BETA-44 closeable), §12 approval closeout.
